steps.title: Spot the grammatical structure
steps.content: The sentence has a main clause: These extensive ______ reflected the poet's evolving literary perspective. The blank must complete the noun phrase These extensive ______ and then allow the list of examples (the addition of hundreds of new poems, the removal of some…) to be offset by commas without creating a second competing verb.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• "changes, including": Including is a nonfinite present participle that opens a supplementary phrase offset by commas, letting the main verb reflected stand alone. Grammatical and clear. ✓
  • "changes would include": Would include is a finite modal verb that creates a second main clause, making the sentence ungrammatical — reflected can no longer serve as the main verb. ✗
  • "changes included": Included is a finite past-tense verb with the same problem: two competing main verbs (included and reflected) in a single independent clause. ✗
  • "changes, include": Include is a finite present-tense verb, equally ungrammatical for the same reason as C. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
